Here view the Mapping Virtual To Internal System and check the configuration settings, the result should be reachable.

From the Edit Settings you can review the configuration settings.

Here the Port numbers are for HTTP - 80XX  and HTTPS – 443XX, where XX is your application server instance number

Make sure resources of the on-premise system are accessible with the Access Policy name “Paths and All Sub-Paths”

  1.  Your Work Zone, standard edition site is created

Execution Steps -

Create a Service Key for the SAP Build Process Automation Instance

Service keys allow you to create credentials for direct access to a specific service instance. By generating and configuring these keys you enable secure and authenticated communication between your service instance and external clients. This includes local development environments, applications in different deployment spaces, or external systems outside your cloud deployment.

Once configured, these keys provide the necessary permissions for these entities to interact with your service thus streamlining integration and access management across various environments.

  1. Login to  SAP BTP subaccount, under Services à Instances and Subscriptions select the instance you created for process automation and select the three dots àselect Create Service Key.

Once created, select View the key.

Once done, download this service key file.

These credentials are used to create a destination for SAP Build Process Automation

Configure SAP Build Process Automation Destination

To set up SAP Build Work, standard edition with SAP Build Process Automation, we will now start by creating an SAP BTP Destination for your subaccount. This Destination serves as a connection point, enabling SAP Build Work to communicate seamlessly with SAP Build Process Automation.

  1. Choose Connectivity> Destinations> New Destination.

Here we will enter the credentials downloaded from our Service Key File.

 

Field

Value

Name

sbpa_wz_destination

Type

HTTP

Proxy Type

Internet

Authentication

OAuth2ClientCredentials

URL

<"endpoints"."api">

Client ID

<"uaa"."clientid">

Client Secret

<"uaa"."clientsecret">

Token Service URL

<"uaa"."url"> add /oauth/token

Note – Please do not use capital letters or special characters other than ‘_’ while providing the name of the Destination. And use the same Destination name as provided.

Additional Parameters -

Note - When copying the values -saasregistryenabled  sap.cloud.service and sap.cloud.service.alias . Please select the values without the quotes.

Field

Value

AppgyverEnabled

true

BuildApps.RequiresCsrf

true

endpoints

endpoints copy the whole JSON structure including { and }

html5-apps-repo

html5-apps-repo copy the whole JSON structure including { and }

HTML5.DynamicDestination

true

saasregistryenabled

Saasregistryenabled

sap.cloud.service

sap.cloud.service

sap.cloud.service.alias

sap.cloud.service.alias

sap.processautomation.enabled

True

WebIDEEnabled

true

 

Perform Check Connection for the Destination.

You should receive this response message "401: Unauthorized"

Create My Inbox Application in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

Once the destination setup is completed, we will now navigate to SAP Build Work Zone Application.

  1. Navigate back to SAP BTP Cockpit subaccount. Choose Services then Instances and Subscriptions. Choose Go to Application next to SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition.

A new application is now opened in another window.

  1. From the main screen navigate to Channel Manager and reload channel HTML5 Apps, the content updates and the status changes to Updated.

If the Destination is configured correctly, here you will receive no error while reloading HTML5 Apps.

  1. Select Content Manager. On the top tabs select Content Explorer and then select HTML5 Apps.

 

  1. Select HTML5 Apps à from the drop down menu of applications select My Inbox and click Add.

  1. Return to Content Manager and select Everyone item to edit it.

Now Add My Inbox Application to Everyone Role.

Once this is saved your My Inbox Application is added to your Work Zone Site.

  1. Create a new group and assign the application My Inbox to the group

In Content Manager a Create new Group

Provide the Name of the new Group and a Description. Here you will have to add My Inbox Application to the group and Save.

Now  when you navigate to your SAP Build Work Zone Site , the new Group and My Inbox Application is available.

Note - To ensure that workflow scenarios display correctly in the My Inbox application on a Work Zone site, you need to set the Asynchronous Module Loading parameter to NO. This adjustment is necessary for accurate and complete scenario display in the application. https://me.sap.com/notes/0003527309

Select Settings and Edit the Site Parameterà Asynchronous Module Loading value as NO and Save.

Additional Key Steps

  1. Make sure to add the following key roles to your user ID in BTP Subaccount  https://help.sap.com
  • Launchpad_External_User
  • Launchpad_Admin
  • Launchpad_Advanced_Theming
  • UAA_Token_Exchange
  1. Check Cloud Connector Destination in SAP BTP

 

Thank You.

entry.title: The Path Forward: SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
SAP Workflow Management and SAP Intelligent RPA Are Retired: Here’s Your Next Step with SAP Build Process Automation 

As part of SAP's continuous innovation, SAP Build Process Automation has been introduced as the successor to SAP Workflow Management within the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). With the recent announcement of the retirement of SAP Workflow Management and SAP Intelligent RPA, now is the time for customers to plan their transition to SAP Build Process Automation. This next-generation tool is designed to bring powerful, unified capabilities for process automation, enabling you to build workflows, deploy automations, and enhance productivity across your organization.

Why Transition to SAP Build Process Automation?

With the retirement of SAP Workflow Management and SAP Intelligent RPA, SAP Build Process Automation has emerged as the comprehensive tool for creating intelligent workflows and automations. Designed to bring together workflow, RPA, and AI-driven process automation, SAP Build Process Automation enables business and IT teams to work collaboratively to simplify complex, repetitive tasks and drive efficiency like never before. 

If you're currently using SAP Workflow Management or Intelligent RPA, now is the time to explore how SAP Build Process Automation can empower your teams with its advanced capabilities. This transition also ensures ongoing support, access to the latest features, and alignment with SAP’s innovation roadmap. 

Join Our Exclusive Migration Webinars 

To help you make this transition seamless, we’re hosting a series of exclusive webinars. Our product management team will provide guidance on migrating to SAP Build Process Automation and answer any questions. You’ll gain insights into: 

  • Migration Strategies: How to move from SAP Workflow Management and Intelligent RPA to SAP Build Process Automation smoothly. 
  • Advanced Features: Explore new capabilities for workflow, RPA, and AI within the SAP Build environment. 
  • Best Practices: Get practical tips and resources to accelerate your migration journey. 

Mark your calendar and choose the session that fits your needs: 

SAP Workflow Management Migration Webinar                             SAP Intelligent RPA Migration Webinar 

📅Date: Tuesday, December 3, 2024                                                     📅  Date: Thursday, December 5, 2024 

⏰Time: 10 am EST                                                                                ⏰Time: 10 am EST   

[Register Here]                                                                                         [Register Here] 

 

Additional Resources 

To further support your migration journey, we’ve curated helpful resources: 

  • SAP Workflow Management - The Path forward
  • Transition to SAP Build Process Automation  
  • Blog post on SAP Community  

Don’t miss this opportunity to learn how SAP Build Process Automation is transforming the automation landscape. Whether you’re in business or IT, these webinars are designed to give you the insights and support you need to make a successful transition. 

Let’s make automation simpler, smarter, and more powerful with SAP Build Process Automation. Register today to unlock the full potential of SAP Build for your organization!

entry.title: Integration of ABAP world in SAP Build
entry.content:
As you might have heard the big announcement from Michael Ameling in this year's SAP TechEd, we are now bringing ABAP world and SAP Build together. If you missed, please check out his blog, where he has provided a little bit of history and the context of integration.

This Blog would cover how these two worlds SAP Build and ABAP are coming together. We will show how if you are a Build developer or ABAP developer or if you want to extend your ERP solutions with ABAP you would be able to so now starting from the Build Lobby.

Why do we want to integrate?

Before we get into creating a package from SAP Build, it would be great to know the reasons for this and the improvements it would bring to the developer community.

1> SAP Build is to become the Entry point for all Developers whether you are a professional developer who loves to code or you are a business user or citizen developer who would like to build applications with minimum to no coding.


SAP BuildSAP Build Portfolio

 


With SAP Build Process Automation we provide a way to automate business processes and  workflows etc. With SAP Build Apps we help you developer wonderful mobile and web apps. With SAP Build Code we enable professional developers to create wonderful applications using Java and JavaScript. 
Now with ABAP also integrated, professional developers are now able to create ABAP packages directly from the SAP Build lobby and start their ABAP development from there.

2> Fusion Development - We know that development work cannot be done in silos. We need to have all the capabilities offered, so that developers could decide how best to achieve a business outcome. With this thought, here in SAP Build when you are working on ABAP, you will not be just developing ABAP packages, but also be actively using it across all SAP Build in a seamless integration.

Fusion Development

 

 

With BAS Service Center, you would be able to integrate ABAP services. When developing UI applications for RAP services, you can now directly open SAP Build Code from the service binding preserving the context of the service and system. This improves the integration of the backend RAP services with frontend Fiori Applications.
You could also wrap the ABAP RAP service bindings as an Action and integrate it in SAP Build Process Automation or SAP Build Apps to automate or build mobile apps with a ABAP Cloud service built in SAP Build.

This ABAP integration is not just for BTP ABAP Cloud, but even S/4 HANA Cloud systems could be accessed and worked upon from SAP Build. 

3> ERP Extensibility - SAP Build is the solution for all S/4HANA Cloud extensibility solutions. Through SAP BUILD Extensibility Wizard, users can now  extend Custom Process, Custom Field and Custom Logic covering both on stack and side by side extensibility. With ABAP integration, this extensibility feature becomes even more powerful. 
Users can now build and extend ABAP services directly from S/4 HANA applications via SAP Build and integrate it to develop their custom extensions.

SAP Extensibility with ABAP

 

Now you know the reasons, let's now get into development. But before that make sure you complete the following pre requisites:

Prerequisites: 

  1. You've subscribed to one of the services in SAP Build. For more information, see What Is SAP Build?.
  2. You have downloaded one of the latest milestone from here and install ADT from the customer update site (https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/latest). Make sure you have selected Eclispe+Command in Link Handlers settings for ADT.
  3. You've created a destination in the SAP BTP cockpit. Please follow the following steps for BTP ABAP and S/4 HANA public Cloud:
    1. Destination for SAP BTP ABAP Environment 
      1. Entitle SAP BTP ABAP Environment to your Global Subaccount; https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-btp-abap-environment/abap-environment/getting-started-with-customer-account-in-abap-environment?locale=en-US&version=Cloud 
      2. Create a Service Instance: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-btp-abap-environment/abap-environment/creating-abap-system?locale=en-US&version=Cloud 
      3. Setup Destination in BTP to connect from BTP to ABAP System

        1. Destination Properties
        • Update additional properties:

         


        It should look like:

    2. Destination for S/4 HANA Public Cloud
      1. Prerequisite: You should have a connection to S/4 HANA Public Cloud system. 
      2. Create a Destination as above with same properties. Only changes are:
        In the url section suffix -api after the system host For ex: my3xxxxx-api.s4hana.ondemand.com
        In audience section add the exact system url without -web or -api.

  4.  Establish a communication system to establish connectivity from ABAP system to BTP
    1. In the SAP BTP cockpit, download your subaccount-specific trust certificate by navigating to Connectivity --> Destinations -->  Download Trust.
    2. In the SAP Fiori launchpad of your ABAP environment system, create a communication system by using the Communication Systems app. See How to Create Communication Systems.
    3. Update Technical Data
      1. For  BTP ABAP Environment  
        1. Navigate to Destination Service
          1. Check “Use Default Instance”.
          2. Update Name by selecting the name of destination configured in Step 3 configured above.

        2. Navigate to Identity Provider
          1. Choose SAML Assertion Provider and update the details:
            1. User Id Mapping Mode: UserName
            2. SAML Bearer Issuer: Upload the Destination trust certificate downloaded above, copy the CN value and paste in the input field.
          2. Save the above changes.
            It would look like this:
        3. Communication System
      2. For S/4HANA Public Cloud 
        1. Navigate to General
          1. Check for Inbound only
        2. Navigate to Identity Provider
          1. Choose SAML Assertion Provider and update the details:
            1. User Id Mapping Mode: UserName
            2. SAML Bearer Issuer: Upload the Destination trust certificate downloaded above, copy the CN value and paste in the input field.
          2. Save the above changes


  5.  You have connected to ABAP System from ADT :
    1. BTP ABAP Cloud  
    2. SAP S4HANA CLOUD 

 

Build your first ABAP service in Build 

ABAP project creation :

In BTP, for any of Build Subscription, click on "Go to Application", it will open a Build Lobby page 

Click on Create Button, it will open a Creation Wizard, select "Build an Application" and then "ABAP Cloud" from the given options:

On Selection of ABAP Cloud:  Project Creation wizard would appear:

The System Dropdown would show the list of Destinations which you have connected to BTP ABAP Environment or S/4 HANA Public Cloud. Make a selection of the Destination which is pointing to your desired ABAP System.

If the destination is valid you will see the System Id and System Client below the System's Dropdown

For a valid System you have two options:

a> You can use a preexisting package which you have already created via ADT 

On Clicking on next you would be directly moved to Build ABAP project creation dialog, details below

b> You can create a new package for a given superpackage:

On Clicking  of next, you will be asked to either enter a preexisting Transport Request  or enable creation of new Transport Request:

If you know the Transport Request, you could select it from the drop down. 
If not, then you could provide the description, a new Transport Request would be generated at the time of package creation.

After clicking on next, Build ABAP Project Creation Dialog would appear. 

This  is Build ABAP Project, This will map and point to the ABAP package which you are creating for a given system. This way you will have a Build ABAP project always referencing to an ABAP package.

In a new tab you will get a prompt to open ADT:

On Selecting "Open Eclipse" another prompt would come to run Eclipse Command , please select OK.
It wil then go to your installed ADT and asked to select an ABAP project 

It will then Open ABAP Repository Object Generator Wizard for ODATA Ui Service 

If you have a reference object, you can select and press Next and proceed or if you want start fresh , you could Cancel.  
You will find the package is created in ADT for your system 

You can now work on the ADT package as you would have done earlier.


ABAP Project navigation

In Lobby page you will also have a Build ABAP Cloud project with the name provided above already created :

 If you click on the ABAP project which you have created it will navigate and open the same referenced ABAP package in ADT 


ABAP Project reference object mapping 

 You can also map existing SAP BUILD ABAP Cloud project to an existing Service Binding object for future reference . 
Click on the Build ABAP project , it would open a Project Details pane . You can click on the Reference Object Name dropdown, if you have an existing Service Bindings for the the ABAP package , you will see them in the list.
Select one.



After the property is updated, it will now update the navigation link. It will now point to the Service Binding which you have selected. Clicking on the project would open the Service Binding Object in ADT:

 

Conclusion 

Now you have build your first ABAP package via build. You can now build any service bindings. As part of fusion development these services could be used across all Build products. If you have a need for extension using these Service , you can use it too. 
More on these in subsequent blogs.

Stay tuned for many exciting developments in this area. We are just getting started.

entry.title: How to scrape images from Google
entry.content:
Someone asked me how to scrape images from Google. I took it as a challenge.

Note that I did an image search, and took the Base64 versions of the images from the search page, which is how Google displays them. These are small, maybe 300-pixels square. If you wanted larger images, you'd extend the automation to click on the image to open the side panel, and use the right-click menu to save/download the image (my next challenge 😺). 

  1. Open Google.
  2. Enter your keyword in the search bar (the keyword comes from an input parameter).
  3. Click somewhere to get rid of the dropdown that hides the search button.
  4. Click "Google Search".
  5. Now on the results page, click "Images".
  6. Wait – before I put this 1s wait, my automation was quite unstable, sometimes worked, sometimes didn't. I believe not all the elements were in place at the beginning so waiting made sure they were loaded.
  7.  
  8. Iterate over all the pictures. I declared as an element anything with class = YQ4gaf – this was contained in the img tags.
  9. I retrieved the src property for the img tag – which was the image in Base64.
  10. I decoded the base64.
  11. I wrote the file to the path that was sent as an input parameter.
  12. Finally I set a condition so I would only loop over the first 10 images (well, 11 since I set the condition wrong 😮.

I then created a process with a trigger form with 2 inputs, the keyword for the Google search and the local path to store the pictures. But I could almost as easily create a Automation Launcher and User Task so I could trigger it attended whenever I wanted. 

 

How I Put It Together

Above looks like I knew what I was doing. But it took a little while, especially the following parts:

  • Recording the screens – this was BY FAR the most complicated part for me. I've tried the tutorials here and here, and hey are great, but I am still really not sure of myself on how the recording of screens works and the selecting of elements. Bu I'm getting there. I will work on it and create some videos on the opic.
  • Decoding Base64 – I was not sure how this was done, since we have documentation on using ctx library to do encoding/decoding, but I soon figured out how to use the Decode activity. I was already a little familiar with Base64 from SAP Build Apps, for which I created a blog on handling images, including in Base64 format.
  • Simulating Enter Key: This I did not even try. I would have needed it because when you enter a keyword the "Google Search" button is hidden, but online people said this was an issue. Instead, I simply clicked the Google logo to get rid of the dropdown that hid the button, and then used a click activity on the search button. 

 

Recording Screens

I did a recording by opening up Google in a new browser, and selecting Create → Application and selecting Recorder (not manual capture) and it created 2 screens – the Google home page, and then the results page.

On the results page, I created 2 captures. The main results page, and then the results page for images.

I also declared some elements I needed, like the search box and button. Most importantly, I declared the list of images, using the class as a criteria, and setting this to a collection.

 

Automation and Process

The automation is created for you, based on the recording, but I found there were all kinds of extra artifacts that I needed to delete or combine. Not a big deal, just something you have to do.

And then I had to add the part where we iterate through the images, decode the Base64 strings, save them to a file, and add a condition to just take the first 10 images. All using simple activities. I also created an input parameter for the keyword, instead of the hard-coded "cat" I used in the recording.

I created the artifacts for enabling this as an attended automation, creating:

  • Automation Launcher
  • User task (for entering the keyword and path)

The automation launcher you would have to register in the Control Tower for your environment.

And the user task you would have to add to your automation. Here is an example from my SAP CodeJam demo of sending emails using an automation.

entry.title: SAP Advanced Financial Closing: Leveraging Workflows to Extend Entity Closes
entry.content:
Background

You are implementing or already using SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC). As part of automating the month-end closing process, you want to extend your entity close in a way SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC) doesn't support out of the box. With the latest incremental release of SAP Advanced Financial Closing in November 2024, we allow to integrate workflows, reflecting processes defined in SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA), with SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC). This also allows you to include SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) bots into your entity close process by calling them within a workflow.

With that you can extend your entity close process e.g. by 

  • two step approval workflows,
  • approval workflows with dynamic determination of the approver,
  • robotic automation by calling a bot within the SBPA process.

Getting Started: A Step-By-Step Guide

Assuming that you have a license for SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA), you can achieve the integration of workflows as follows:

1. Set up SAP Build Process Automation Instance

Where: BTP Cockpit

First, if not yet done, you need to set up an SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) instance. 

Note: Currently, the instances of SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) and SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC) are required to be in the same BTP sub-account.

2. Connect AFC to SAP Build Process Automation Instance

Where: SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC), App "Specify Communication Systems"

We now connect SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) to SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC). For this, the following steps are required:

  1. Create a new communication system of type SAP Build Process Automation System.

  2. Click "Create". Then, the respective communication system is available.

Note:

  • The current status of the SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) instance can tracked in the "Monitor Communication Systems" app.
  • Once created, connection check and navigation to SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) is possible by using the "Monitor Communication Systems" app.
  • Value helps and actions in SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC) related to SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) are visible only if a system of type SAP Build Process Automation System was maintained.
  • Columns in SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC) related to SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) are by default hidden but available in the respective table settings.

3. Defining Workflows in SAP Build Process Automation

Where: SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA)

A thorough documentation on SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA), and in particular how to create and maintain workflows, can be found on SAP Help Portal: SAP Build Process Automation.

As a result, you have created a project in SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) containing processes that are reflected as workflows in SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC). 

4. Include Workflows in AFC Templates

Where: SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC), App "Manage Closing Task Lists"

It's now possible to include tasks of type "Workflow" in templates and task lists as follows:

  • Create a task from workflow via action "Create Task from Workflow".
  • Select one of the workflows via value help. 
  • Click "Create".

  • The result is a new task of type "Workflow" with basically the same attributes (and thus behavior / functionality) as for ABAP jobs.
    Additional attributes: External Job ID (which is the workflow ID)
    Attributes not applicable to workflows: Remote Processing User

Note:

Value Help

  • All workflows maintained in the connected SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) system are shown here, there's no (hard-coded) filtering.
  • The available workflows are directly retrieved from processes defined in the connected SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) system.
  • There is no duplicate persistence in SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC).

Parameters

  • A predefined set of parameters is mapped automatically (see SAP Help Portal: Integration of Tasks of Type Workflow).
  • Other parameters can be maintained manually.
  • Arrays are not yet supported (planned to be improved in one of the next releases).
  • Relative dates (rules) are not yet supported (planned to be improved in one of the next releases).
  • Dates are converted into supported type (DATE).

5. Start Workflow

Where: SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC), App "Process Closing Tasks"

The workflow added to the template (and thus to generated task lists), can then be started as follows:

  1. Schedule the workflow.

  2. Check the scheduling details.

Note: Workflows behave similar to ABAP jobs regarding

  • planned start / duration,
  • start mode,
  • executions (split per company code), and
  • processors (which are also mandatory).

Workflows behave different to ABAP jobs:

  • Only SENT parameters are available as sent & used parameters are always the same for workflows.
  • "Remote Processing User" is not supported.

6. Processing Workflows

Where: SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA)

A thorough documentation on SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA), and in particular how to create and maintain workflows, can be found on SAP Help Portal: SAP Build Process Automation

Note: In order to get (steps of) a workflow in the SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) inbox, this step needs to be assigned to the respective SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) user.

7. Check Workflow Results

Where: SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC), App "Process Closing Tasks"

As soon as the workflow was finished, this reflects in the status of the respective task:

  • Status
  • Output parameters
  • Logs

Note:

Status

Currently, we only show the "technical status" of the workflow process:

  • Completed without errors (workflow runs through)
  • Failed (workflow run fails)

Handover of business status planned in one of the next releases.

Retry Mechanism

  • It's possible to retry technically failed workflows on SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) side. This might change the status of the task from "Failed" to "Completed w/o Errors" even though the task was already started again on SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC) side.
  • The SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) log of the task might be extended (by the messages from the retry). 

Output Parameters

  • Output parameters handed back via JSON (chunked if required)
  • Planned to be improved in one of the next releases.

8. Check Reporting

Where:  SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC), App "Closing Task Completion"

The workflows can of course also be checked in the reporting apps (filter e.g. for task type "Workflow").

Summary

Using SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) workflows, you can extend your entity close in a way SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC) doesn't support out of the box.

For more information on SAP Advanced Financial Closing, check out the following links:

    • SAP Community Page
    • SAP Help Portal
    • SAP Roadmap Explorer
    • SAP.com Page

Follow us via #AdvancedFinancialClosing, SAP Advanced Financial Closing, or myself via @ThomasLuecking.

Disclaimer

Please note that SAP Advanced Financial Closing gets frequent updates and enhancements. Therefore, the current features and functions may not be exactly as described here. For the latest information, you can refer to the product documentation on the SAP Help Portal.

Please note that the information given here or any related document and SAP’s strategy and possible future developments, products and or platforms directions and functionality are all subject to change and may be changed by SAP at any time for any reason without notice. The information in this document is not a commitment, promise, or legal obligation to deliver the functionality described here or any related material or code. Instead, the information is for informational purposes only. It may not be incorporated into a contract. SAP assumes no responsibility for errors or omissions in this document, except if such damages were caused by SAP’s intentional or gross negligence.

Update Q4/2023: The official product name of AFC has been simplified from SAP S/4HANA Cloud for advanced financial closing to SAP Advanced Financial Closing. This doesn’t affect neither the strategy nor its functionalities, it is just a new product name.

entry.title: SAP runs SAP: Insights into Financial Closing Transformation with SAP AFC
entry.content:
Financial Closing Transformation

As financial operations grow in complexity, organizations increasingly seek to optimize their financial closing processes. SAP Advanced Financial Closing (AFC) has emerged as a robust solution to enhance these operations, bringing automation, streamlined task management, and greater transparency to the closing cycle. This blog delves into our transformation journey through SAP AFC, exploring key steps from initial template cleanup to advanced automation and integration efforts. The outcomes have not only improved efficiency but also empowered teams to focus on more strategic, value-adding activities.

FCC Template Cleanup: A Strategic Preparation for AFC Implementation

A crucial early step in our SAP AFC implementation journey was cleaning up templates of the predecessor tool – FCC (Financial Closing Cockpit), an often overlooked but vital task. Over time, tasks were added to the FCC, many of which became obsolete due to evolving processes, yet they remained active in the system. As a result, certain teams either never used these tasks, or they were inconsistently activated, causing confusion over the true status of the financial closing.

This overload made it difficult for stakeholders to get a clear view of progress. Therefore, our initial focus was a thorough review of over 20,000 tasks in the FCC. Each unique task was assessed for its relevance, current use, and necessity. This meticulous review allowed us to filter out redundant tasks and focus on the critical ones, eliminating unnecessary clutter in the system and enhancing the overall closing process.

Following the cleanup, we identified opportunities to replace or improve outdated automations. In many instances, tasks previously performed manually were transformed into fully automated processes. This initial streamlining laid the groundwork for more sophisticated automations and faster financial closing cycles, ensuring that only essential tasks entered the AFC framework.

Replacing Third-Party Automations with SAP iRPA: Efficiency and Cost Reduction

One of the significant challenges in modern financial departments is managing diverse automation tools. Before implementing AFC, we relied on third-party automation platforms, which, while functional, came with high operational costs and limited scalability. SAP’s native automation tool, SAP SBPA (SAP Build Process Automation, previously SAP intelligent Robotics Process Automation), proved to be a game-changer, offering deep integration with SAP environments, and reducing external dependencies.

In a relatively short period, we developed, tested, and deployed multiple SAP iRPA bots dedicated to various financial processes. These bots now handle essential tasks such as bank reconciliation preparation and the analysis and posting of general bad debts.

We currently operate eight unattended bots in SAP iRPA, each designed to execute specific financial operations autonomously. These bots have streamlined processes, eliminated manual intervention, and substantially reduced the time required to complete routine tasks. The shift from third-party to SAP automation not only simplified our tech stack but also led to measurable cost reductions by consolidating our tools within the SAP ecosystem.

Simplifying Authorization Management and SCIM Interface Integration

Effective role and authorization management is vital for maintaining security and operational efficiency, especially in complex financial environments. Prior to implementing SAP AFC, our authorization management processes, while functional, required streamlining to improve efficiency and ease of use. Roles were often unclear, and responsibilities were scattered across various departments, leading to inefficiencies in task execution and oversight.

The integration of the SCIM (System for Cross-domain Identity Management) interface into SAP AFC introduced a new level of simplicity and precision to our authorization processes. This integration allowed for the automatic provisioning and de-provisioning of users based on their roles, making it easier to manage user access and control.

The new role structure under AFC is much clearer and more effective, aligning specific tasks with precise static and instance authorizations such as end-user, support, or administrator. Additionally, scoped roles further refine access by limiting user permissions to specific areas, such as country-specific operations or department-specific tasks. This ensures that users only have access to what is necessary for their roles, reducing the risk of unauthorized access. This shift not only improved operational clarity but also enhanced security by reducing the risk of unauthorized access. The streamlined approach to authorization management also facilitates quicker onboarding for new team members and minimizes downtime related to role changes.

Task Structure and Management Optimization: Building for Efficiency

One of the standout features of SAP AFC is the ability to create Task List Models in the backend, which simplifies the administration and management of financial closing tasks. This capability allowed us to develop a new structure for task management, linking closing tasks to our financial closing timetable. The timetable is accessible as a high-level overview within AFC, giving managers and stakeholders an intuitive view of the entire closing process.

As a result, task management has become significantly more organized and accessible. This task management system not only streamlines task administration but also ensures logical connections between tasks, making them easier to track. Through the "Manage Closing Task Lists" app, authorized users can oversee the closing structure, manage dependencies, and task parameters, and effortlessly generate new task lists.

Monitoring task execution times and error rates has been another critical aspect of this transformation. By analysing this data, we can make real-time adjustments to the process, optimizing execution times and improving user experiences. This continuous improvement cycle has helped us minimize delays, address bottlenecks promptly, and ensure that our financial closing runs smoothly.

Expanding Automation: Continuous Collaboration and Innovation

Automation has been central to our AFC transformation, but our efforts didn’t stop at standard automations. By collaborating with different finance teams and other departments, we continuously identify new use cases for automation. This collaborative approach has allowed us to expand the scope of AFC beyond its original framework.

One of the key benefits of AFC is its flexibility. We’ve used this to our advantage by enabling teams to easily review the results of automated tasks and monitor their progress. Rather than introducing new manual tasks, we have focused on extending automation to cover more scenarios. This has been particularly useful for high-volume, repetitive tasks, where automation reduces the risk of human error and accelerates completion.

A key feature that has facilitated this expansion is the use of task dependencies, which allow for the automatic triggering of subsequent tasks once preceding tasks are completed. This has enabled us to create process chains that execute with minimal intervention, further reducing the burden on our teams.

Better Task Organization and Responsibility Distribution Across Finance Departments

A key improvement in our financial closing processes has been the enhanced organization and clarity of tasks. By adopting a standardized naming convention and utilizing custom fields, we can now easily identify which financial area each task pertains to. This improved organization has simplified task management across the department, making it easier to assign and track responsibilities.

In the past, hundreds of users were involved in triggering and monitoring individual tasks, which required significant coordination. With AFC, we have shifted to a more centralized approach, where a smaller group of users is responsible for specific tasks. By using user groups, we have reduced the need for widespread user involvement, freeing up valuable resources to focus on higher-value activities. This simplification has been a game-changer, allowing us to concentrate on more strategic tasks rather than being bogged down by the execution of repetitive, low-value processes.

Significant Error Reduction and Faster Closing Cycle

One of the most significant results of our AFC implementation has been the dramatic reduction in error rates. By reviewing each task and continuously developing improvements, we’ve been able to eliminate many common issues that previously plagued the financial closing process.

Today, manual tasks are primarily reserved for documentation, approval, or compliance-related checks, with most tasks now fully automated. This shift has not only improved accuracy but also shortened the overall financial closing cycle.

A key feature supporting this transformation is the Financial Closing Overview and Closing Task completion apps within AFC, which provides real-time insights into the status of the closing process. Users can monitor both the general progress and individual tasks with ease, allowing them to make timely decisions and adjustments. Proper notification scenarios ensure that users are always informed about task status, further enhancing the efficiency of the closing process. The current automation rate in AFC-integrated systems is around 88%, indicating high operational efficiency. Additionally, the error rate for automated tasks has been significantly reduced and stabilized at approximately 5%, well within the acceptable failure criteria for optimal performance.

The Future of AFC: Expanding Automation with Group Reporting Integration and AI-Guided Error Resolution

Looking ahead, we are planning to integrate Group Reporting with AFC, marking a new chapter in our financial closing journey. This integration will bring even greater transparency and efficiency to our financial reporting processes, allowing us to further extend the scope of automation and process optimization. In addition to this, we are currently testing and improving our new solution for Gen AI-guided error resolution in AFC, which promises to significantly enhance the speed and accuracy of identifying and resolving issues.

In conclusion, SAP AFC has been instrumental in transforming our financial closing processes. By reducing manual tasks, enhancing automation, simplifying task management, and improving user experiences, we’ve not only streamlined operations but also positioned our teams to focus on strategic, value-adding activities. Our AFC journey is ongoing, and as we continue to innovate and expand its scope, including advancements in AI-driven solutions, we look forward to achieving even greater operational excellence in the future.

entry.title: New Videos on DMS with SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
I have published 2 short videos on the integration of SAP BTP Document Management Services with SAP Build Process Automation.

The first video explains how to connect DMS to SAP Build Process Automation, so that the File widget is available to add to forms.

The second video explains how to add the File widget to a form, how you can add an uploaded document to another form, how the document is represented by a text string (this is important when connecting to automations), and how the document is stored in the DMS repo.

These videos are follow-ups to the 3 Tech Byte videos on setting up DMS, and explaining the 3 forms of DMS: Integration, Application and Repository options.

entry.title: Architecting Email Approval without My Inbox for SAP Build Process Automation Workflows: Part 1
entry.content:
Business Context

While working day in and day out on SAP BTP use cases, we have noticed a growing inclination among customers towards “Automation” specially with SAP BTP SAP Build Process Automation. This trend has led to the development of various side-by-side custom extensions on SAP BTP utilizing SAP Build Process Automation.

Problem Statement

One of the most common requests we receive from multiple customers is for a mechanism that allows email-based approval without the need to manually log into the My Inbox application.

Currently, there is no out-of-the-box capability available to fulfill this requirement. Therefore, we will be developing our own custom module to address this need.

In this three-part blog series, my colleague & BTP Architect @Adwait_Fadnavis  and I will provide insights on how to implement Email-based approval using SAP Build Process Automation capabilities.

We have prepared the content for this series in three blogs, where we will be diving deep into Design, Development, and Testing.

  • Blog 1 (This blog) – This blog will focus on the Business process, context, problem statement, end to end development flow, and required prerequisites.
  • Blog 2 This blog will focus on development and will provide a detailed walkthrough of the development process.
  • Blog 3: This blog will focus on complete end-to-end testing, helping to understand the overall objective.

Scenario Explanation

In this scenario, a workflow will be triggered using SAP Build Process Automation, which will send an email notification to the respective approver.

“The approver will receive the email notification and can reply to this email with either “Approve” or “Reject.” 

Based on the response, the corresponding workflow task will be approved or rejected.

Important Considerations

  1. Valid Approver Verification: The approver must be a valid approver, and the email must come from the approver’s registered email address. If the email does not come from the proper approver, or if the response is not “Approved” or “Reject,” the system must send an email back indicating that the response is not valid.
  2. Unique Request Number: Each approval request must generate a unique request number. This number will be used to validate the response against the original request, ensuring that the approval or rejection is correctly matched to the corresponding workflow task.

Disclaimer:  We can't recall the email after the approval/rejection.

End to End Business Process Flow: Streamlining Approval Processes with SAP BPA Email-Based Automation

End to End Process Flow : Email based Approval

  1. Workflow Initiation

The process begins when an SAP BPA workflow is triggered. This could be due to various business events, such as a purchase order requiring approval.

  1. Sending Approval Work Items : Once triggered, the workflow sends approval work items to the approver’s SAP BTP My Inbox. Simultaneously, an email notification is dispatched to the approver.
  2. Email Notification Details :The email notification includes essential details like the “workflow instance ID” or “Custom Request Number” in the subject line. This ensures the approver & automation (which will elaborate later) can easily identify the request.
  3. Approver’s Response Options: The approver has two options to respond:
  • Log into SAP BTP My Inbox: Approvers can log in and approve or reject the request directly.
  • Email-Based Response: Approvers can reply to the email with “Approve” or “Reject.”

Background Automation

An SAP BPA-based automation runs continuously in the background, monitoring the responses sent by the approver. This automation checks if the response is from a valid approver and corresponds to a valid work item.

  1. Updating Work Items: If the response is valid, the automation updates the SAP BPA work item accordingly. This eliminates the need for the approver to log into My Inbox separately, streamlining the process.
  2. Completion Notification: Upon completion of the process, an approval email is sent to confirm the outcome. This ensures all stakeholders are informed of the decision.

Benefits of Email-Based Approval Automation

  • Efficiency: Reduces the time required for approvals by allowing responses directly via email.
  • Convenience: Approvers can respond from anywhere without needing to log into the system.
  • Accuracy: Automated monitoring ensures only valid responses are processed, reducing errors.

Initial Setup and Configuration for Inbound SMTP-Based Approval

Implementing an email-based approval system using SAP Build Process Automation (BPA) requires a few essential setup steps. Below steps will walk you through the initial configuration to ensure a smooth and efficient process.

Outbound SMTP Destination in SAP Build Process Automation

To send emails from SAP Build Process Automation to users’ email accounts, you need to configure an outbound SMTP destination. This setup is crucial for enabling the system to dispatch approval email notifications.

Inbound SMTP Email Configuration

For receiving email responses from approvers, we will use Google for both inbound and outbound SMTP. This setup can also be adapted for Microsoft email or other email services. Here are the key steps:

  1. Enable Access for Reading Gmail: Ensure that the Gmail account used for receiving responses has the necessary permissions enabled. For detailed instructions, refer to the relevant  sap documentation.

Save Credentials JSON File: After setting up the Google Cloud Platform(GCP), securely save the credentials. These credentials are necessary for the SAP BPA to access and read incoming emails.External User Setup in SAP BPA Control Tower: Complete the configuration for the external user in the SAP BPA Control Tower. This step ensures that the system can correctly route and process incoming emails from approvers.

SAP BTP BPA Control Tower

 

Setting Up External Authentication for the Google(Gmail)

Desktop Agent Installation and External User configuration (SMTP Inbound)

The Desktop Agent must be installed in unattended mode to run background processes efficiently.

It is recommended to install the Desktop Agent on a virtual machine to ensure continuous operation. Follow these steps:

Desktop Agent Sub Account Setup: Configure the Desktop Agent within the appropriate sub-account.

  1. Set to Unattended Mode: For real-time production scenarios, set the Desktop Agent to unattended mode. This setup is crucial for automating the approval process without manual intervention.
  2. Configure External User: Ensure that the external user configured in the previous step is correctly set up in the Desktop Agent. This configuration allows the agent to process email responses and update the SAP BPA work items accordingly.

Desktop Agent - Registering External Authentication

By following these steps, you are all set for robust email-based approval system using SAP BPA, streamlining your approval processes and enhancing efficiency.

Design and Development Walkthrough for SMTP Inbound Email-Based Approval Automation 

Once the initial setup and configuration are complete in the SAP BPA Control Tower and the unattended Desktop Agent is installed & configured, the next step is to design and develop the automation components.

Below is a summary of the key components involved in this process. My colleague @Adwait_Fadnavis  will provide a detailed walkthrough in a subsequent blog post.

The first step is to create a BPA automation. This involves several key actions:

  1. Invoke Google Authentication and Search for New Emails:

    • Authenticate External Gmail: Set up Google authentication to allow the system to access your Gmail account.
    • Search Gmail: Create a step to search for new, unread emails with the appropriate subject and request ID. Ensure that only unread emails are read to avoid processing duplicates.
    • Validate the Approver:

    • Check Valid Approver Validation : Validate that the response is from an authorized approver by reading the subject line for the request number.
    • Fetch Work Item Details: Use the SAP BPA API to retrieve work item details and match the approver. If the approver is not valid or authorized, send an error message.
    • Read the Email Body and Response:

    • Process Response: If the email response contains “Approve” or “OK,” call the SAP BPA API to update the workflow task accordingly.
    • Send Completion Email: Notify Stakeholders: After successfully processing the workflow approval or rejection, send an email notification to inform all relevant parties of the outcome.

In the upcoming blog post, @Adwait_Fadnavis  will delve into the development flow in detail and provide valuable insights on how to effectively develop inbound SMTP. Final Development flow chart will look as below:

End to End: Development FlowStay tuned for a comprehensive guide that will enhance your understanding and streamline your Development  process.

Regards,

Mohit Bansal, SAP BTP & S4HANA Architect

entry.title: Architecting Email Approval without My Inbox for SAP Build Process Automation Workflows: Part 3
entry.content:
This is the third and final blog of the series, in which we would like to explain the testing part in detail.

We have kept testing separate in this blog to provide a comprehensive explanation of all scenarios and steps from a technical perspective.

We have prepared the content for this series in three blogs, where we will be diving deep into Design, Development, and Testing.

  • Blog 1 – This blog will focus on the Business process, context, problem statement, end to end development flow, and required prerequisites.
  • Blog 2 This blog will focus on development and will provide a detailed walkthrough of the development process.
  • Blog 3(This blog) : This blog will focus on complete end-to-end testing, helping to understand the overall objective.

In the previous blog, @Adwait_Fadnavis  walked you through the complete development process. Now, I would like to start with the testing part.

Detailed Example of the Testing Process

To illustrate the testing process in more detail, let’s walk through an example:

  1. Automation Scheduling: The automation is scheduled to run every 15 minutes. The desktop agent is set up on a virtual machine to ensure it runs in unattended mode.
  2. Dummy Workflow Setup: A dummy workflow is created with fields such as “Request ID,” “Requester Name,” and “Approval Status.” Upon submission, an email is sent to the approver.
  3. Email Response Handling: The approver receives the email and responds with “Approve.” The automation script checks the email subject line and sender’s email address to validate the response.
    1. Invalid Response / Invalid Approver Handling: If the response is not valid or from an unauthorized approver, the system logs the incident and notifies the relevant parties for further investigation.
    2. Approver Handling: If the response is valid or from an authorized approver,  Workflow task will be approved.
    3. Rejection Scenario: If the approver responds with “Reject,” the workflow status is updated to “Rejected,” and an email notification is sent to the requester and other stakeholders.
  4. Workflow Update: The workflow status is updated to “Approved,” and an email notification is sent to the requester and other stakeholders.
  5. Sending Notification Emails: Email Notification to all stake holders involved.

Let's Deep dive above steps in Detail:

Step 1: Automation Scheduled as Background Job

In a real-time scenario, this automation can be scheduled as a background job at intervals of 15 minutes, 30 minutes, or any custom-defined time interval.

The frequency can be adjusted based on the volume of approval tasks. This scheduling ensures that the system checks for new approval requests regularly and processes them in a timely manner.

As mentioned in the first blog, it is mandatory to have the desktop agent running in unattended mode. This setup is crucial for ensuring that the automation runs smoothly without manual intervention.

For real-time business scenarios, we recommend installing the desktop agent on virtual machines to ensure continuous operation and to handle high volumes of approval tasks efficiently.

Job scheduling : SAP Build Process Automation

Step 2: (Optional) Triggering the Workflow -  Prepare the Dummy Workflow for Quick Testing

To facilitate quick testing, we prepared a small workflow using SAP Build Process Automation with a few fields. This dummy workflow simulates the real-time process and helps in validating the automation setup. Although in real-time, the workflow will be triggered via a valid application, here is a snapshot of our dummy workflow for demonstration purposes:

  • Workflow Trigger: Upon clicking the submit button, the workflow is triggered.
  • Email Notification: An email is sent to the approver for approval. This step involves configuring an SMTP outbound email, as mentioned in the previous blog post.

This setup allows us to test the end-to-end process in a controlled environment before deploying it in a live scenarioriggering the Workflow -  Prepare the Dummy Workflow for Quick Testing (Optional)

Test for Sending Email for Task Creation

Sample Email Body

Triggering Workflow : Testing Demonstrations:

Process Triggered and Sent for Approval

 Step 3: Handling Email Responses

Upon receiving the email, the approver will have three options: a) Approve b) Reject c) Not a valid response

Let’s say the approver starts with “ok” and responds with this in the email.

Upon receiving the response, our automation running as a background job will check if the response is coming from a valid approver and if the subject line is correct (the subject line should not be modified).

This validation step is crucial to ensure that only authorized responses are processed.

Validating Correct ApproverValidating Correct Approvers Response will be send if the Approver is not valid.

Response will be send if the Approver is not valid.

Validating Correct response: Validating Correct response

Step 4: Workflow Update on Approval

If the work item is approved, the workflow will move forward and update the task accordingly. This involves:

  • Updating the Workflow Status: The status of the workflow is updated to “Approved” in the system

Workflow Update on Approval

  • Notifying Stakeholders: All relevant stakeholders are notified about the approval via email.

Notifying Stakeholders

Workflow Update on Rejection

Similarly, if the response is “no” or “Rejected,” the work item will be updated, and the workflow will proceed with the rejected branch. This involves:

  • Updating the Workflow Status: The status of the workflow is updated to “Rejected” in the system.

Updating the Workflow Rejection Status

  • Notifying Stakeholders: All relevant stakeholders are notified about the rejection via email.

Step 5: Sending Notification Emails

An email notification will be sent to all involved parties to inform them of the status of the workflow request. This step ensures transparency and keeps everyone informed about the progress of the approval process.

rejection via email

We hope you enjoyed reading our blog series by me and @Adwait_Fadnavis  on Architecting Email Approval for SAP Build Process Automation Workflows. We trust that this approach provides valuable insights into managing workflow approvals efficiently without relying on manual My Inbox / Task Center approvals.

Thank you for following along with us.

Regards,

Mohit Bansal l, SAP BTP & S/4HANA Architect , Linkedin

entry.title: SAP Build 中的新增功能 – 2024 年第 3 季度版本
entry.content:
本文为翻译文，原文链接 What’s New in SAP Build – Q3 2024 Edition, 原作者 EsmeeX。

你好，开发者！ 我们很高兴为您介绍 SAP Build 第 3 季度的新增功能系列。 在此版本中，我们将介绍针对以下内容的产品特定更新：

  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Work Zone、SAP Start、SAP Task Center
  • SAP Build Code

SAP Build Apps

版本管理和项目生命周期管理：现在可以使用 SAP Cloud Transport Management 系统跨不同租户发布和传输项目。在隔离 SAP Build Apps 应用程序到其自有架构的开发、测试和生产部署时，您可以在子账户之间传输项目版本。要开始，请参阅此博客帖子。

包含 S/4HANA 中大型数据集的性能表列表：管理和显示应用程序中的大量数据可能具有挑战性并导致性能问题。查看此示例了解如何为具有 65k+ 记录的 S/4HANA Cloud 端点创建性能列表页面 - 请参阅博客帖子。

在一个项目中复制页面：此功能使用户能够在 SAP Build Apps 的项目中复制和复制页面，这是一个简单但功能强大的扩展组件，可进一步加快开发速度。

新的构建服务：我们最近推出了全新的改进体验来部署应用程序包。构建服务包括管理图像资产、iOS 使用字符串、移动服务启用、打包创建和配置管理。更新的 UI 在部署阶段为开发人员提供了更顺畅的体验，确保满足每个要求并且您的应用已准备好发布。试试吧！

  • 另请查看路多·诺恩斯 (Ludo Noens) 系列视频，了解如何借助 SAP Build Apps 使用 SAP 解决方案，创建 CRUD 应用
  • 详细了解 SAP Build Apps 路线图的最新动态。

SAP Build Process Automation

SAP Build Extensibility Wizard for SAP S/4HANA Cloud：SAP S/4HANA 公共云的 2408 版现在提供可扩展性向导。这使得并排扩展的创建更加简化，并允许您从cloud ERP 无缝导航到 SAP Build。第一个版本支持自定义流程创建，SAP Build 今年将提供更多选项。有关详细信息，请参阅博客。

Script Task Editor (脚本任务编辑器)：现在可以在新的脚本编辑器中编写 JavaScript 代码，从而允许在流程上下文中执行复杂的数据类型转换和附加操作。之前，这仅限于自动化 - 现在包括流程输入和自定义变量，有助于高级数据操作和转换。在博客帖子中查看更多信息。

计划触发器：现在，您可以在运行时根据用户定义的时间间隔和日期计划流程自动化。这允许您在创建自动化方案时启用以系统为中心和基于时间的流程自动化用例，例如工作日和非工作日，以避免资源不匹配。

表单中的附加结果和 UI5 任务支持：这使开发人员能够在流程中配置附加结果操作，以便在审批表单中缺少信息或信息错误时让审批人请求返工。您还可以通过引导式体验有效地开始填写多个表单。

通过手动说明上传创建高级事件项目：这允许用户上传业务事件规范，以在大厅中创建事件项目。新功能增加了对跨业务范围 (LOB) 和自定义事件的业务事件的支持，包括在运行时使用 SAP Event Mesh 支持的第三方事件。

流程可见性增强：包括切实可行的洞察、针对请求者的实例跟踪以及通过 CALM 提供的附加监控功能。现在，您可以将洞察转化为有意义的结果，并根据可见性洞察触发上报流，以直接响应和解决关键情况。这样可实现可审计和治理的上报解决方案，而不是在不同渠道断开通信。

了解有关 SAP Build Process Automation 的更多信息：

  • 有关更多更新和改进，请参阅 What's New Viewer（新增功能查看器）。
  • 有关更多即将推出的功能和重点区域，请参阅 SAP Road Map Explorer。

SAP Build Work Zone、SAP Start 和 SAP Task Center

SAP Build Work Zone（两个版本）

HTML5 业务解决方案作为内容提供者：通过 SAP Build Work Zone 中的最新更新，您现在可以以“通用数据模型”(CDM) 格式将基于 BTP 的应用程序公开为 Work Zone 内容，例如角色、空间和页面。因此，您可以轻松配置和启用从 Work Zone 实例访问 BTP 应用程序的功能，甚至可以跨子账户访问。 请参阅帮助门户和此博客帖子中的更多信息。

SAP Build Work Zone（标准版）

定义页面和空间的排列顺序：管理员现在可以配置在 SAP Build Work Zone（标准版）的菜单栏上对空间和页面进行排序。仅当在空间和页面的新体验查看模式(new experience view mode)下工作时，此功能才适用。请参阅帮助门户中的更多信息。

页面和空间的导入和导出：将增强内容管理器工具，使管理员能够在架构之间导出和导入自定义（本地创建的）空间和页面。

SAP Build Work Zone（高级版）

跨 SAP Build Work Zone 和 Process Automation 的集成引导式体验：现在，引导式流程可用作 SAP Build Process Automation 中的部件。除了之前可用的“无模板”向导外，现在还可以在 SAP Build Work Zone 中创建基于流程的引导式向导。请参阅博客帖子中的更多信息。

SAP Start

直接导航到集成云解决方案的产品主页：SAP Start 站点提供了一个新产品分区，可以轻松查看和导航到集成解决方案的产品主页。这使当前用户的可用云解决方案透明化，并快速轻松地导航以启动所需解决方案的产品主页。

SAP Task Center

ITCE 合作伙伴页面：SAP Task Center 现在允许与第三方任务提供者连接。利用此功能的第一个解决方案是由 ITCE 与 SAP 架构师和技术顾问合作开发的 TaskConnect。该应用程序很快将在 SAP Store 中提供，提供全面的配置指南、安装手册和 BPMN 图表。有关 TaskConnect 的详细信息，请参阅此处。

有关 SAP Build Work Zone、SAP Start 和 SAP Task Center 的详细信息：

  • 查看 SAP Build Work Zone 标准版和高级版和 SAP Start/SAP Task Center 的路线图
  • 新增功能查看器：标准版/高级版/SAP Start/SAP Task Center 

SAP Build Code

新增的 AI 功能：在 SAP Build Code 中，您现在可以使用 Joule copilot（我们的生成式 AI 助手）通过提供自然语言提示编写代码。关于Joule的详细解释,请阅读此文。在过去几个月中，我们为此功能添加了许多新功能，包括以下亮点：

  • 支持面向数据模型和应用逻辑的 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 服务集成：现在可以使用Joule为集成的 SAP S/4HANA Cloud 服务生成代码。
  • SAP Build Code 中的 Joule 主要用户体验更新：借助 SAP Build Code 内的 Joule 的最新用户体验更新，我们提高了可用性，简化了工作流，并创建了更直观的界面，提高了生产力和满意度。
  • 生产力工具的生成式AI 支持：我们现在允许您直接从故事板使用Joule。
  • 使用Joule增强现有的数据模型和服务：现在可以使用提示修改现有的数据模型和服务实体。

为什么选择 SAP Build Code：查看产品经理 Rupa Maity 发布的博文，了解为什么迁移至 SAP Build Code。

了解最新的构建代码创新成果：您是否希望随时了解 SAP Build Code 的最新产品更新？在 SAP Community 上关注我们的标签，并订阅 SAP Build Code 简报。 

  • 请参阅 SAP Build Code 自 2024 年 9 月起的最新更新产品更新。
  • 了解有关 SAP Build Code 社区的更多信息。

--- 

 

请注意，此博客帖子提供了最新功能和增强的亮点。 

  • 有关所有更新的完整列表，请参阅 Help Portal SAP Build 中的新增内容。
  • 加入 SAP Builders group，获取产品资源和社区问题。
  • 不要错过 SAP Build-themed sessions 等。
  • 这意味着 SAP TechEd 就在眼前！在 2024 年 TechEd 大会上，深入了解 SAP Build。

您最想测试哪些功能？请在评论中告诉我们！

entry.title: SAP & AGILITA Workshop on SAP Build: what a great experience!
entry.content:
At the end of last week I had a very stimulating trip to Berlin, where I participated in the SAP & AGILITA Workshop on SAP Build. It was an interesting event and I'm happy to share my takeaways with you.

Thursday November 27, 8:30 AM: I join my colleagues who arrived from Walldorf and Helsinki for a breakfast at our hotel, and we quickly head up to SAP Berlin office. Once there, together with the Berlin colleagues, we set up the event rooms we had booked for the keynote and then the hands-on workshop.

At 10.30, attendees start to arrive: we have the pleasure to welcome business users and developers from Berliner Verkehrsbetriebe (BVG), Bundesdruckerei, Stiftung Das Rauhe Haus, Hochschule für Technik und Wirtschaft Berlin as well as partners and a few SAP employees. All together, we engage discussions over a buffet of snacks before the event kicks off at 11.00 with our keynote, joined by many attendees on LinkedIn live and moderated by Esmeé.

Keynote and solution deep dive

Diana Roesner starts the keynote by introducing the Clean Core concept and explaining how SAP Build can be used to easily create side-by-side extensions to an SAP application. On this very important topic, I highly recommend you a new white paper - “Clean core extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud” - and a crisp and easy-to consume learning journey - “Practicing Clean Core Extensibility For SAP S/4HANA Cloud”.

We then dive into the different components of the SAP Build solution, with my close colleagues Kanishka presenting SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Code, and Yumnah describing SAP Build Apps and SAP Build Work Zone. Jesse gives a great demo of how SAP Build can help 🎅🏻 Santa Company 🎄 streamline order approvals by creating an extension directly from his SAP S/4HANA ERP using our Extensibility Wizard. Finally Hemant provides an insightful perspective on how SAP Build supports line-of-businesses, including finance, procurement, supply chain, customer experience, human resources, IT and cross-functional:

Following the presentations, our experts held a Q&A session with the audience, answering the questions raised on-site as well as in the online chat:

We received very positive feedback from the audience on this session, which you can watch as a replay.

You can also download the slides of this keynote.

Hands-on Workshop

After a short break, we moved to another room for the hands-on workshop. In 3 hours, our local participants had the opportunity to create a full-stack application to improve customer loyalty. The purpose of the application is to allow customers to redeem points from their purchases. To do this, the application needs to access customer, purchase and product data from SAP S/4HANA.

Specifically, workshop participants experienced using SAP Build Code to create a back-end CAP service with Joule AI copilot, highlighting SAP's generative AI power for development. The customer loyalty application is connected to SAP S/4HANA Cloud as an extension to the ERP, so the participants learned how to use a BTP destination. And they used SAP Build Apps to create a mobile app, showcasing our modern low-code/no-code development tool.

This is the part of the event I was involved in, and I have to say it was great and rewarding to see our participants amazed at how Joule helped them create data entities, sample data and JavaScript code from simple prompts.  They really experienced the power of Generative AI that we showcased at SAP TechEd a few weeks ago!

If you would like to do this hands-on exercise and experience Joule by yourself, it’s very easy!

Indeed, it is available here in our Basic Trial environment, where you will have immediate access to all the tools needed: SAP Build Code - Generative AI-powered development with Joule copilot.

Christmas Market at AGILITA’s

At 16:00 we jumped into a bus who drove us to the AGILITA office in Berlin. There, AGILITA Christmas Market brought everyone together to reflect and share ideas in a festive atmosphere.

Thomas Neuhaus, Head of Digital Experience for AGILITA, presented SAP AppHaus Workshops: SAP and AGILITA are helping customers find the right path to digitalization with design and architecture thinking workshops. Customers discover how to integrate Al and innovation for their own use cases, and how to effectively improve their business processes.

We also had the opportunity to attend very interesting demos of applications developed by the AGILITA team with SAP Build, showing how tasks like recording time or logging work can happen in just 3 clicks:  

AGIL AI.TimeManager  - Optimize your time and activity recording with AGIL Time Manager, the flexible and user- friendly solution for efficient time management and seamless integration in real-time.
➡️ More on AGIL AI.TimeManager in SAP Store.

 

AGIL Al.Sales  - Boost sales performance with the power of voice and Al synergy. Effortlessly dictate orders and meeting notes while on the go - advanced generative Al transcribes, analyzes, and seamlessly updates your systems in real time.
➡️ More on AGIL AI.Sales in SAP Store.

 Discover more AGILITA’s applications and extensions in SAP Store.

Up to you now!

You too can take advantage of the content we have prepared for this event. And it's very simple!

  1. Watch the keynote replay 🎞  ️
  2. Download the presentation 👍🏻  
  3. Do the hands-on exercise 👩🏻‍🎓👨🏻‍🎓

 

Personal note

To end this blog post on a more personal note, I would like to say that I had 2 very energizing days in Berlin. I only work 3 days a week, Monday-Wednesday, but when I was asked if I could attend this event in Berlin on a Thursday, I didn't hesitate for a second: as I work 100% from home in Lyon, it was a great opportunity to meet up with colleagues from Berlin and Walldorf. The day after the event, we took time to reflect on what we did in 2024 and what we would like to do in 2025. This trip to Berlin was also a nice opportunity to meet great people from our valued partner AGILITA.

And when I was on my way back to Lyon on Friday evening, what caught my eye in the Berlin airport terminal?

On a big screen, an ad from SAP promoting our magical trifecta of Process + Data + AI 😊
